title: Eurycaulis speckmaieri
taxonomy: Eurycaulis speckmaieri is a species of coniferous tree in the cypress family, Cupressaceae. It is a genus endemic to Taiwan. The tree is classified as evergreen and coniferous, belonging to the order Pinales. The species Eurya speckmaieri is also known as the 'Taiwan Cypress' and is considered a rare and endangered tree species. Further classification of the species can be found in the kingdom Plantae.
properties: Eurycaulis speckmaieri, commonly known as the Taiwanese yew, has been found to possess potential therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Its extracts have been studied for their potential in treating various diseases, such as cancer and cardiovascular disorders. The plant's leaves and bark contain compounds with cytotoxic properties, which may inhibit the growth of cancer cells. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Eurycaulis speckmaieri.
